description: A faithful Codex pet adaptation of a retro mechanical sea captain with a huge long nose, curled mustache, worn blue captain coat and tricorn hat, brass joints, and stout little metal boots.

idle.notes: Calm watchful captain idle with tiny blink, mustache twitch, and barely-there collar settle.

walk.notes: Short steady deck-patrol walk rows used for both travel and drag-facing behavior.

curious.notes: Thoughtful route-check pose with hand near mustache and a focused inquisitive head tilt.

happy.notes: Restrained proud captain moment with a small pleased gesture and dignified self-satisfaction.

sleep.notes: Standing doze with closed eyes, lowered head, and subtle hat and mustache settling.

lookout.notes: Independent lookout row using a compact brass spyglass and subtle horizon-check motion.

brace.notes: Small alert brace-and-rise motion, more like a tiny deck hop than an athletic jump.

grumpy.notes: Stubborn irritated captain slump and recovery, kept dry and effect-free.

drag.notes: Directional drag uses the true left and right walk rows, not approximations.

lookout.notes: Fixed atlas had no dedicated lookout row, so the running row slot was intentionally remapped to an independent lookout strip.

dragFacingValidation.rule: deltaX < 0 => facing left; deltaX > 0 => facing right
manualVerification: left drag uses a true left-facing strip
right drag uses a true right-facing strip
walk_left and walk_right are not swapped
walk_left head, nose, mustache, coat front, and gait all face left
walk_right head, nose, mustache, coat front, and gait all face right
long nose, tricorn hat, curled mustache, and high collar stay readable at small size
lookout remains a separate row and does not interfere with drag mapping
